reasoning: The unsafe verdict was based solely on battery_status_helper.sh, which is a legitimate applet helper that requires root privileges to function. It doesn't execute during makepkg and poses no supply-chain risk.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen incorrectly flagged battery_status_helper.sh as unsafe due to its CPU governor functionality, while Deepseek correctly recognized it as a benign runtime component.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped reviewing the source tarball (expected)
Qwen failed to review critical build components like make_translate_template.sh while Deepseek did
false_positives: Qwen's battery_status_helper.sh verdict: Misinterpreted legitimate sysfs access as unauthenticated manipulation (requires root)
learnings: Improve model training: Clarify distinction between build-time risks vs runtime functionality
Enhance file prioritization: PKGBUILD hooks and build scripts should take precedence over applet helpers
Add context guardrails: Prevent over-alerting on sysfs/device files that require elevated privileges
Improve reasoning transparency: Models should explicitly state why runtime components fall outside audit scope
